content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

April 26, 2001

Edwin P. Brennan, M.D.
540 Montecito Boulevard
Napa, California 94559

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-00-092

Dear Dr. Brennan:

Pursuant to my letter of February 16, 2001, I am treating your request for advice as withdrawn. I hope the information regarding post-government employment that I enclosed with the letter was helpful.

If you have any further questions, please contact me at my direct number: (916) 322-5783.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: C. Scott Töcher
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

LM:CST:jg

F:\DATA\LEGAL\ADVICE LTRS\00092.WPD

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

February 16, 2001

Dr. Edwin P. Brennan
540 Montecito Blvd.
Napa, CA 94559

Dear Dr. Brennan:

Regarding your inquiry to this office about potential post-employment restrictions after your departure from state service, I have been unable to secure the necessary information from the pharmaceutical companies you have referenced about their dealings with the Department of Mental Health. This information must be provided by you in order to secure formal advice. While with an incomplete factual picture I am unable to provide formal written advice, I enclose a document in the meantime that provides detailed guidance on the rules governing post-employment restrictions. I enclose also a copy of your disclosure category identifying the scope of the inquiry into the involvement of these firms with the Department.

I will phone you on Wednesday, February 21, to discuss the information with you and attempt to answer any questions you may have. Also at that time, if you have acquired the facts regarding Curry, Martin, Schiavelli, Falk Communications, and the World Health Continuing Medical Education firms' dealings with the Department of Mental Health, I will be happy to incorporate that information in an advice letter.

Of course, if you wish to speak before the 21st, or wish to schedule another time, please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5783.

Very truly yours,

C. Scott Toeher,
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
